title: refactor buildah types and structs from heavy deps
body:
When other pieces of code, like the go bindings in libpod, import buildah structs and types, they do not want to also have the burden of importing containers image and containers storage.  To avoid this, we put these structs into builld/define/run|build.go.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

/kind other

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:

@nalind @cevich we're having lint issues across a couple of different PR's that I don't think is due to changes in the PR's, this PR included.  The error message is not at all enlightening, any ideas?

This needs to add [aliases](https://golang.org/doc/go1.9#language) for types that it intends to move, in order to avoid breaking our API consumers, and aliases for the constants as well.

@TomSweeneyRedHat It looks like the linter's getting SIGKILLed, so my first guess would be the OOM killer in the environment where it's running.

...oh yeah, joy, lovely, ***that*** problem.  To be more specific...I've seen similar "it just dies" problems before, been frustrated to no end about them, and never ever arrived at any meaningful conclusions :sob: 

What I learned:  golangci-lint is VERY memory and CPU hungry.  If it runs out of memory, it will be killed like this.  If it warms up the CPUs too much, it will be killed like this.  If a rat chews on a network cable somewhere in the world...yep, it will be killed :confounded: 

Give this a try, it made a difference with a remarkably similar problem in containers/podman CI:

```diff
diff --git a/.cirrus.yml b/.cirrus.yml
index 59cbdad3..be190255 100644
--- a/.cirrus.yml
+++ b/.cirrus.yml
@@ -97,6 +97,9 @@ gce_instance:

     timeout_in: 30m

+    gce_instance:
+        memory: "16Gb"
+
     setup_script: '${SCRIPT_BASE}/setup.sh |& ${_TIMESTAMP}'
     build_script: '${SCRIPT_BASE}/build.sh |& ${_TIMESTAMP}'
     validate_test_script: '${SCRIPT_BASE}/test.sh validate |& ${_TIMESTAMP}'
```

If that doesn't do it, and it's still failing tomorrow, please let me know.  I'll likely need to don the latex gloves, double-N95 masks, get my big hammer, and confirm my Mom is on the speed-dial.

> so my first guess would be the OOM killer in the environment where it's running.

Methinks a very good first-guess :smile:

@nalind @cevich, ty and I feel better about the loss of my hair from the afternoon.
As one of my PR's was also bit, #2677, I've added the `.cirrus.yml` change to it.

Tried to compile this PR against podman got two errors.

pkg/api/handlers/compat/images_build.go:172:35: undefined: buildah.IsolationChroot
cmd/podman/images/build.go:452:47: undefined: buildah.NamespaceOption
